content.problemStatement: Given **n** words **w**[**1**..**n**], which originate from the same stem (e.g. grace, graceful, disgraceful, gracefully), we are interested in the original stem. To simplify the problem, we define the stem as the longest consecutive substring that occurs in all the **n** words. If there are ties, we will choose the smallest one in the alphabetical (lexicographic) order.
constraints: All words consist only of lower‑case English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- A line with an integer n, the number of words.
- A line with n lower‑case words separated by a single space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the stem – the longest consecutive substring that appears in every word. If several substrings have the same maximal length, output the lexicographically smallest one. If no common substring exists, output an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The longest substring appearing in all four words is "grace".
Common substrings of maximal length are "int" and "ent" (length 3). "ent" is lexicographically smaller, so it is chosen.
No character is common to both words, therefore the stem is the empty string.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input: 1\n10\nabcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst abcdefghijklmnopqrst

solution.approach: For each test case we first pick the shortest word because any common substring cannot be longer than it. Then we enumerate all its substrings (O(L^2) where L is its length). For each candidate we check whether it occurs in every other word using the standard library substring search (O(L) per check). While enumerating we keep the best substring: longer length is preferred, and for equal length the lexicographically smaller one is kept. The total work per test case is at most O(L^2 * n * L) which is tiny for L ≤ 20 and n ≤ 10.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L^2 * n * L) per test case, where L ≤ 20 and n ≤ 10. In practice ≤ 8·10^4 operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) for storing the current substring and the best answer.
